Univariate Descriptive Analysis of Preprocessed Data - Flight Prices

General Statistical Overview

Information of all variables
Variable Type NAs
flightDate flightDate Date 0
startApt startApt factor 0
destApt destApt factor 0
elapsedDays elapsedDays numeric 0
economy economy factor 0
nonStop nonStop factor 0
taxAmount taxAmount numeric 0
totalPrice totalPrice numeric 0
seatsLeft seatsLeft numeric 0
travelDistance travelDistance numeric 0
departure departure POSIXct/POSIXt 0
arrival arrival POSIXct/POSIXt 0
airline airline factor 0
equipment equipment factor 322
segmentDistance segmentDistance numeric 0
layoverNumber layoverNumber numeric 0

Comparing this table to the raw dataset, we can see the impact of our preprocessing steps. Initially, variables like travelDistance and segmentDistance had 404 missing values. After the KNN imputation process, these variables have no NAs. However, equipment still shows 322 missing entries because our imputation method focused on numerical variables, leaving categorical fields as they were.

Statistical Summary of Numeric Variables
Variable Min Median Mean Max SD CV
elapsedDays elapsedDays 0.00 0.00 0.14 1.00 0.35 2.46
taxAmount taxAmount 15.95 44.32 47.88 192.93 20.51 0.43
totalPrice totalPrice 33.97 299.60 330.73 837.19 168.77 0.51
seatsLeft seatsLeft 0.00 7.00 6.06 10.00 2.90 0.48
travelDistance travelDistance 97.00 1473.00 1631.93 4173.00 854.94 0.52
segmentDistance segmentDistance 79.00 720.00 901.83 2722.00 638.68 0.71
layoverNumber layoverNumber 0.00 1.00 0.82 3.00 0.60 0.72

The statistics clearly show the effect of our outlier treatment. For instance, the maximum totalPrice dropped from 2570 USD to 837 USD, and the travelDistance maximum was also adjusted. This confirms that the IQR filter successfully removed the extreme values that were distorting our data. Additionally, the decrease in the Coefficient of Variation (CV) for totalPrice (from 0.58 to 0.51) indicates that our dataset is now more consistent.

Variable: taxAmount

Variable: totalPrice

The preprocessing phase improved this variable. By applying IQR-based clipping and KNN imputation, we removed the extreme outliers that were distorting the mean. The distribution is now much more representative of the typical flight price. If we compare the boxplot after preprocessing with the one before, we can clearly see this improvement.

Variable: seatsLeft

The distribution remains uniform with specific inventory peaks at 7 and 9 seats.

Variable: travelDistance

The chart shows two clear groups: short and long flights. Since we filled in the missing data during preprocessing (imputation process), this variable is now complete and reliable for our analysis.
